message:
bd-3bxhj.8.2: Fixture taxonomy and multi-dimensional coverage analysis

Adds fixture_taxonomy module for classifying corpus entries across 7
coverage dimensions (UI patterns, state, effects, styling, accessibility,
terminal, data flow) with 73 total dimension values. Includes blind spot
detection, overrepresentation analysis, and complexity scoring.

Also fixes clippy issues in ir_normalize.rs (unused imports, unused
variable, map_entry pattern).

17 tests passing, clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1484,7 +1484,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-3bxhj.3","title":"epic(opentui-import): Canonical IR and semantic normalization","description":"## Background
Normalize extracted semantics into a canonical IR that captures intent independent of OpenTUI implementation details.

## Scope
- IR schema and invariants for state, interaction, layout, style, and effects.
- Lowering + normalization passes with deterministic ordering/idempotence guarantees.
- Intent graph inference (layout/focus/interaction).
- Effect canonicalization into deterministic command/subscription semantics.
- IR versioning/explainer tooling plus unit/E2E determinism validation.

## Success Criteria
1. IR passes are deterministic and idempotent for fixed inputs and configs.
2. Every normalized artifact is provenance-linked to upstream extraction evidence.
3. Effect and async semantics map cleanly into deterministic runtime abstractions.
4. Ingestion-to-IR E2E traces are replayable with stage-level hashes.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:28:19.821714463Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T22:22:55.372741464Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compiler","ir","opentui-import"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:28:19.821714463Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3bxhj.3.1","title":"task(opentui-import IR): Design canonical migration IR schema and invariants","description":"Objective: Define canonical IR capturing view tree, state graph, event transitions, effects, style intent, capabilities, and accessibility semantics independent of source syntax.\n\nAcceptance criteria:\n1. IR schema is versioned and machine-validated.\n2. Invariants are explicit (acyclic ownership, deterministic ordering, stable IDs).\n3. Schema supports provenance links back to source constructs for diagnostics.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:30:01.555940220Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T04:23:42.642835264Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T04:23:42.642814205Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ir","opentui-import","schema"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:15.022376055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:15.486655395Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:30:01.555940220Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3bxhj.3.4","title":"task(opentui-import IR): Infer layout, focus, and interaction intent graphs","description":"Objective: Infer higher-level intent graphs (layout constraints, focus traversal, interaction clusters) needed for high-quality FrankenTUI generation beyond syntax-level translation.\n\nAcceptance criteria:\n1. Inference outputs confidence-scored intents with explainable evidence.\n2. Ambiguous intent paths are preserved as alternatives for planner decisions.\n3. Focus and interaction graphs are compatible with ftui-runtime event model.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:30:03.128587342Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:19.960142977Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["inference","ir","opentui-import"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:30:03.128587342Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:19.960070892Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3bxhj.3.5","title":"task(opentui-import IR): Canonicalize effects and async boundaries into deterministic command/subscription model","description":"Objective: Convert extracted effect semantics into a deterministic IR form aligned with FrankenTUI command/subscription execution boundaries.\n\nAcceptance criteria:\n1. Effect ordering and cancellation boundaries are explicit.\n2. Retry/backoff/timer semantics are represented without hidden side channels.\n3. Model supports deterministic replay and certification diffing.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:30:03.717644198Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:20.420156576Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["effects","ir","opentui-import"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:30:03.717644198Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:20.420080975Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3bxhj.3.6","title":"task(opentui-import IR): Implement IR versioning, migration adapters, and schema compatibility checks","description":"Objective: Support controlled IR evolution with migration adapters and compatibility validation so historical artifacts remain analyzable.\n\nAcceptance criteria:\n1. Schema changes require explicit migration plan and compatibility tests.\n2. Older manifests can be upgraded reproducibly to current IR version.\n3. Version mismatch errors include actionable upgrade guidance.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:30:06.250802951Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:21.338405944Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ir","opentui-import","versioning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:30:06.250802951Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:20.883317953Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:21.338343046Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -1549,7 +1549,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-3bxhj.8.17","title":"task(opentui-import optimization): Build golden-output checksum oracle and isomorphism-proof artifact pipeline","description":"Objective: Guarantee behavior preservation for optimization changes via golden outputs, checksum validation, and structured isomorphism proof documents.

Required outputs:
- Golden-output capture pipeline over representative fixtures with checksum manifests.
- Isomorphism proof template compiler (ordering, tie-breaks, floating-point, RNG seed invariants).
- Verification gate requiring golden-check success before optimization promotion.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Any optimization change that alters behavior without declared policy exception fails the gate.
2. Proof artifacts link directly to change_id, fixture set, and checksum verification results.
3. Logs include proof_id, invariant status, checksum verdict, and counterexample replay command on failure.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:16.832443907Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:51.185291953Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extreme-software-optimization","isomorphism","opentui-import"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.17","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:50.176144106Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.17","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:51.185247900Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.17","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.6.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:50.667821658Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.17","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:16.832443907Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.17","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:49.710819806Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3bxhj.8.18","title":"task(opentui-import optimization): Enforce one-lever-per-change and rollback-readiness policy","description":"Objective: Prevent optimization drift by enforcing one lever per change window and requiring explicit rollback plans with post-rollback verification steps.

Required outputs:
- One-lever policy checker over optimization manifests and change metadata.
- Rollback-plan schema with revert command, risk level, and post-rollback validation commands.
- Gate integration blocking multi-lever or rollback-incomplete optimization submissions.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Multi-lever changes are rejected unless policy override artifact and risk waiver are attached.
2. Every accepted optimization includes rollback metadata and post-rollback verification checklist.
3. Logs include change_id, lever_count, override_flag, rollback_plan_id, and rollback_readiness verdict.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:17.279341960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:53.084407857Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extreme-software-optimization","opentui-import","rollback"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.18","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:53.084344619Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.18","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:17.279341960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.18","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:51.709868636Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.18","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:52.175783021Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.18","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.9.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:52.627326774Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3bxhj.8.19","title":"task(opentui-import optimization): Implement iterative re-profile loop and bottleneck-shift historian","description":"Objective: After each accepted optimization, re-profile automatically, detect hotspot migration, and update the optimization backlog using measured deltas.

Required outputs:
- Post-change re-profile trigger with before/after metric comparison.
- Bottleneck-shift detector that re-ranks opportunity matrix candidates after each round.
- Optimization history ledger tracking round number, change, metric deltas, and new hotspot frontier.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Every optimization round writes a before/after delta report with p50/p95/p99 and hotspot movement.
2. Backlog candidate scores are recalculated after each round using latest profile data.
3. Re-profile failures or unstable measurements trigger conservative pause with triage hints.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:17.796588696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:54.447699975Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extreme-software-optimization","opentui-import","profiling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.19","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:17.796588696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.19","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:53.995302804Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.19","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.18","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:53.537584748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.19","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:54.447640694Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Every fixture is annotated across declared coverage dimensions.\n2. Coverage scoring can identify blind spots and overrepresented patterns.\n3.  {"id":"bd-3bxhj.8.20","title":"task(opentui-import optimization tests): Unit/property tests for baseline/profiler/scoring/isomorphism/one-lever loops","description":"Objective: Add comprehensive unit/property tests for the optimization-control plane and behavior-preservation gates.

Coverage requirements:
- Baseline statistic aggregation, percentile computation, and command fingerprint stability.
- Hotspot extraction determinism and opportunity-matrix score math correctness.
- Isomorphism proof validation, checksum-oracle behavior, and one-lever policy enforcement.
- Rollback-plan schema validation and re-profile backlog recalculation logic.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Unit tests cover happy path, malformed artifacts, missing evidence, and adversarial metric outliers.
2. Property tests verify deterministic outputs for fixed seeds and fixture manifests.
3. Failure logs include run_id, hotspot_id, score_terms, proof_id, lever_count, and replay command.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:18.253621960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T22:34:09.205819765Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extreme-software-optimization","opentui-import","test","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.20","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:57.882168498Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.20","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:33:13.235528875Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.20","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:34:09.205778719Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.20","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:18.253621960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.20","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:54.994895437Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.20","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:55.515391428Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.20","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:56.038135058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.20","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:56.512075834Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.20","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.18","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:56.966847873Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.20","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.8.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:31:57.423731486Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3bxhj.8.21","title":"task(opentui-import optimization tests): E2E optimization gauntlet for profile-first, one-lever, isomorphism, and rollback","description":"Objective: Build an end-to-end optimization gauntlet that executes the full loop: baseline -> profile -> score -> single-lever change -> isomorphism verify -> re-profile -> rollback drill.

Gauntlet scenarios:
- Positive path with measurable p95 improvement and unchanged golden outputs.
- Red paths: low-score rejection, multi-lever rejection, checksum mismatch, unstable profiling, and rollback failure.
- Drift path: post-change hotspot shift that reorders next optimization candidates.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Pipeline emits deterministic JSONL logs for each loop stage with artifact linkage and replay commands.
2. CI gate fails on any violated optimization-policy invariant or behavior-regression proof failure.
3.  {"id":"bd-3bxhj.8.22","title":"task(opentui-import optimization): Implement tiered optimization round escalator (Round1/2/3) with hard eligibility gates","description":"Objective: Operationalize multi-round optimization strategy so advanced algorithmic/exotic techniques are only attempted when lower-tier opportunities are exhausted or justified by evidence.

Required outputs:
- Round policy engine (Round 1 low-hanging, Round 2 algorithmic, Round 3 exotic) with explicit entry/exit criteria.
- Eligibility checker using latest opportunity matrix and hotspot evidence.
- Audit trail for round transitions, including skipped-tier justifications and risk annotations.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Round transitions require machine-verifiable evidence that prior-tier Score>=2.0 options are exhausted or rejected with rationale.
2. Advanced-round candidates include stronger proof/rollback requirements before activation.
3.
